content: Claude Code sessions have two distinct IDs: (1) Claude's session_id (UUID from SessionStart/SessionEnd input) and (2) coordination session_id (generated by session-register). These require mapping via claude_session_id column in sessions table.
notes: Observation about how session IDs work in the system

content: When using Set<string> for multi-select tracking in Vue, mutations require full replacement to preserve reactivity. Example: selectedIds.value = new Set([...selectedIds.value, id]) instead of selectedIds.value.add(id).
context: Vue reactivity for multi-select with Set<string>
notes: Recurring Vue reactivity pattern, not a one-time decision

content: When committing spec changes, must run spec-convert variations script before committing. Organize commits as: (1) Core code changes, (2) Generated artifacts + spec updates together, (3) Optional separate commits for config changes.
context: spec conversion workflow and commit organization
notes: Workflow preference for commit organization

content: E-commerce taxonomy design pattern: Consolidated overly granular subcategories into broader categories with filter attributes. Example: 5 separate Factory Ammunition subcategories merged into single category using caliber as filter attribute.
context: Inventory feeds e-commerce category taxonomy design
notes: Specific technique applied to solve a taxonomy problem

content: Phase 2 Admin Page classes vs Facades decision: PR #7 created modular Admin page classes. PR #8 was based on older develop branch and when merged, it DELETED all Admin page classes because Phase 1 used Facades architecture.
context: sermon-browser architecture evolution and merge conflicts
notes: Merge deleted work due to architectural mismatch

content: Database migrations and schema definitions don't require TDD (they're configuration/infrastructure, not business logic). TDD applies to parser logic, API handlers, and business rules.
notes: Guideline about where TDD applies vs not

content: Premortem identified missing feature: When using external API services with rate limits, implement client-side usage tracking to warn users before exhausting quota.
notes: General guideline about API rate limit handling

content: For autoEndHours computed property in pharmacokinetics app: numDays should be derived from current endHours value (Math.ceil(endHours/24) + 1) rather than being fixed.
notes: Specific implementation approach for computed property

content: Pattern for multi-item auto-calculation: When extending single-item auto-calculation to support multiple items, use Math.max() pattern: prescriptions.map(p => calculateForOne(p)).reduce((max, val) => Math.max(max, val), defaultValue).
notes: Reusable code pattern for array calculations

content: When implementing complex tasks with 20+ subtasks in a TDD project: (1) Use task-master-ai for details, (2) Break into phases, (3) Implement core MVP first, (4) Follow TDD principles, (5) Ensure quality.
notes: Recurring workflow pattern for task management

content: The hook-auto-execute rule says to execute bash commands when hooks block with deny + command. However, when a hook recommends using a skill or Task agent instead of providing a direct bash command, the auto-execute rule doesn't apply.
notes: Pattern about when a rule applies vs not

content: The memory daemon had a bug where sessions were marked as extracted in the database immediately when queued, not when extraction actually completed. This meant failed extractions prevented retry.
notes: Identifies bug symptom (premature marking) and root cause (mark on queue not completion)

content: Before implementing a task, verify dependencies are actually complete by checking git history, not just task status fields. Tasks marked Pending in metadata may have commits showing completion.
notes: Recurring verification pattern

content: When creating XCTest files on disk before wiring them into the Xcode project, SourceKit reports No such module XCTest - this is expected. The error resolves itself once files are added to the Xcode project.
notes: Specific error diagnosis - false positive explained

content: In BID (twice daily) dosing with accumulation, the peak concentration keeps growing with each dose until steady state is reached. The global peak can occur late in the simulation because later doses accumulate on top of residual concentrations.
notes: Domain-specific behavioral pattern in pharmacokinetics

content: When committing spec changes: always run the spec-convert script BEFORE committing. Spec source changes should be committed ALONG with regenerated artifacts.
notes: Workflow preference for commit process

content: npm audit can show stale vulnerability reports if node_modules hasn't been updated. After updating package.json, must run npm install before npm audit reflects changes.
notes: Specific error diagnosis with fix steps

content: In Vue component tests with Vitest, partial Prescription objects cause Cannot read property of undefined errors in computed properties. Test setup must provide complete valid objects matching the interface.
notes: Specific error with diagnosis and fix

content: When updating WordPress posts via REST API, the content field replaces the entire post content. To append new sections, fetch existing content first. The raw field in GET responses may be null - use rendered field instead.
notes: API behavioral observation — describes how it works, not an error fix

content: Silent error swallow anti-pattern: using try? context.save() in clearQueue() discards save errors without logging, while drain() correctly wraps save in do/catch. Always use do { try ctx.save() } catch { log } not try? for SwiftData saves.
notes: Prescriptive rule: always use do/try/catch, never try?

content: When WordPress abilities have no parameters, the properties field is serialized as empty array [] instead of empty object {}. The MCP adapter expects a record (object). Root cause is PHP serialization behavior.
notes: Specific error with root cause analysis

content: When PHPUnit test file is updated but tests still fail with old values, the issue is PHPUnit cache. Fix: Clear PHPUnit cache or re-run tests to pick up file changes.
notes: Specific caching error with fix

content: The /commit skill is designed to create commits WITHOUT Claude attribution. Never manually add Co-Authored-By: Claude lines to commit messages.
notes: User preference about commit attribution

content: Using replace_all to change slot references like slots.get('unit') to slots.get('temperatureUnit') will incorrectly update ALL intents, including ones that should still use 'unit'. Make targeted edits per intent, not global replacements.
notes: Approach that was tried and failed

content: Using wp eval is an antipattern to be avoided when testing fa-wpmcp abilities. Instead, test through the actual MCP interface or use proper test scripts.
notes: Approach identified as antipattern

content: When generating multiple images/graphs back-to-back, each image consumes significant context space. For large analysis sessions, estimate context usage proactively and create a handoff BETWEEN tasks.
context: Context management during large multi-output sessions
notes: Sequential generation approach that caused context overflow

content: In-memory queue state is lost on daemon restart. If 200+ sessions were queued but never processed due to zombie processes blocking the queue, restarting clears them all. Need persistent queue or better zombie handling.
notes: Architecture approach that failed under real conditions

content: HTML entities like u are NOT decoded by WordPress - they display as literal entities. Zero-width spaces break commands when users copy-paste. Both approaches fail for creating functional copy-pasteable code examples.
notes: Two approaches tried and both failed

content: Using Cmd+Q to test crash recovery doesn't work because it triggers a graceful shutdown. On macOS, Cmd+Q sends SIGTERM allowing SessionEnd hooks to fire. Use kill -9 to simulate a true crash.
notes: Testing approach that didn't work as expected

content: Refactoring Vue tests to remove 'as any' casts can break working tests. Tests that were passing (20/20) suddenly fail completely (0 passing) after linting-motivated TypeScript improvements.
notes: Refactoring approach that broke tests

content: BinBrain: the live Raspberry Pi database is behind 5 migrations. Missing: photo_detections, photo_detection_groups, photo_group_items, photo_labels tables. Before deploying to Pi, run all files in migrations/ in order.
notes: Incomplete work that needs to be done

content: VOYAGE_API_KEY is set in dotfiles but EMBEDDING_PROVIDER is not set anywhere (defaults to local). Existing ~2022 learnings stored with BGE local embeddings will have poor search results if queried with Voyage embeddings.
notes: Migration work not yet started

content: mcp-adapter-execute-ability has empty parameter bug: When parameters is {} (empty object), PHP empty() returns true for empty array, converting to null. Fix: change empty($input['parameters']) to !isset() or is_null().
notes: Specific bug diagnosis with identified fix

content: Cache/Maintenance abilities still fail via MCP despite fixes. Direct PHP execution works. MCP returns generic error with no PHP errors logged.
notes: Fixes were tried and failed — still broken

content: BinBrain: GET /photos/{photo_id}/suggest never calls detect() - it just returns an empty sorted list. This is an incomplete implementation, not a stub.
notes: Incomplete implementation flagged for future work

content: User has an x86_64 Linux machine: AMD Ryzen 9 9900X 12-core, 32GB RAM. Two GPUs: onboard AMD Radeon and GeForce GTX 1650 SUPER (6GB VRAM). Can run Hailo Dataflow Compiler natively.
notes: Environment fact, not a preference

content: Conditional expects in tests are anti-patterns. Linters flag these because tests should always validate something. Refactor to assert the condition first or restructure the test.
notes: User's preferred testing conventions

content: For sermon-browser project, always use ParaTest instead of PHPUnit for running tests. ParaTest is 2.6x faster (45s vs 117s) using 8 parallel processes.
notes: Tool preference for a specific project

content: Workflow pattern (search-read-edit): Grep -> Read -> Edit.
notes: Too terse to determine preference — reads as observation

content: When user says push to main and current branch is master, the intent is clear: rename branch from master to main, push to origin. However, deleting old master branch is destructive and still requires confirmation.
notes: Describes expected behavior/interpretation rather than a prescriptive rule

content: User requires all git commits to be GPG signed. Never use --no-gpg-sign flag. If GPG signing fails, leave files staged for user to commit manually.
context: git commit workflow and signing requirements
notes: Hard user requirement about GPG signing

content: New features for the fa-wpmcp project should be documented in the README.md file under the Future Enhancements section.
notes: Documentation preference for specific project

content: We do not use the GEMINI_MODEL env variable to change Gemini models. Instead, use the --model flag when invoking the Gemini agent.
notes: User preference about tool configuration

content: Recovered memory_daemon.py code lost to a revert by mining session transcripts from S3 archives. Downloaded 12+ JSONL transcripts, parsed Edit tool diffs using jq.
notes: Creative solution to code recovery problem

content: Vue component test access pattern: Use vm.property directly (not vm.property.value) when accessing refs through component instance. Vue Test Utils exposes refs as already-unwrapped properties.
notes: How Vue Test Utils behaves — observation about the framework

content: Alexa skill simulation showed unexpected error but direct Lambda invoke worked perfectly. The unexpected error was an Alexa-side issue, not a Lambda issue. Always verify Lambda works directly before debugging Alexa integration.
notes: Diagnosed unexpected error — root cause was Alexa-side, not Lambda

content: WordPress Gutenberg blocks use comment-style delimiters: <!-- wp:block-type --> HTML content <!-- /wp:block-type -->. The REST API recognizes block_version: 1 and renders with proper block classes.
notes: How Gutenberg works — observation about the format

content: For large-scale refactoring (70+ files), use a hybrid approach: manually refactor 2-3 files to establish the pattern, then spawn parallel agents to handle different directories.
notes: Strategy that worked for large refactoring

content: To backfill unregistered Claude Code sessions into PostgreSQL: (1) Use JSONL UUIDs as session IDs, (2) Decode project paths from directory names using DFS filesystem resolution.
notes: Technical solution for session backfilling

content: Database-based crash detection approach: Query sessions table for sessions with (1) no exited_at timestamp AND (2) stale heartbeat. This filters out both cleanly exited sessions and concurrent active sessions. File markers are replaced by adding exited_at column to track clean exits.
context: database-based crash detection vs file markers
notes: Chose database over file markers with clear rationale

content: Crash recovery hooks migrated from file-based markers (active-session.json) to PostgreSQL sessions table. The old approach used a single marker file per project — this caused false crash detection when multiple terminals opened the same project.
notes: Explains why old approach failed and why new approach was chosen

content: Testing coverage limited by constructor side effects and tight coupling. Classes with constructor hooks and direct WP_Query instantiation are difficult to unit test fully. Acceptable to document this limitation rather than over-engineering tests.
context: PHPUnit testing WordPress plugins with tight coupling
notes: Decision to accept limitation rather than over-engineer

content: When converting parsed spec objects back to markdown with frontmatter, the original markdown content is lost during parsing. The solution is to re-read the original markdown file when generating .md artifacts rather than trying to reconstruct from the parsed object.
notes: Chose re-read over reconstruct approach with rationale

content: For multi-prescription comparison features, use comparePrescriptions array rather than currentPrescription when computing auto-calculated values. The pattern: check comparePrescriptions.length > 0 first, falling back to currentPrescription only if comparePrescriptions is empty.
context: Vue architecture - form state vs visualization state separation
notes: Chose visualization state over form state for calculations
